name: The temptation in the wilderness
notes: Led by the Spirit into the wilderness, Jesus fasts forty days and is tempted. Mark compresses the whole episode into two verses — the Spirit driving him out, forty days, and the angels ministering — naming no single temptation, so only those beats link to him. Matthew and Luke both give all three temptations with near-identical wording, but in different order: Matthew runs bread, temple, mountain; Luke runs bread, mountain, temple. The links therefore cross between the two — Matthew's second temptation answers to Luke's third — which is why the connectors tangle rather than run straight.

links.label: led of the Spirit into the wilderness
led by the Spirit into the wilderness
If thou be the Son of God, command ... be made bread
Man shall not live by bread alone
all the kingdoms of the world
All these ... will I give thee
Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve
a pinnacle of the temple ... cast thyself down
He shall give his angels charge
bear thee up, lest ... thou dash thy foot against a stone
Thou shalt not tempt the Lord thy God
